Veridian HealthBank, Inc. Data Processing Addendum and MNPI Handling Appendix (US issuer context)
Parties: Veridian HealthBank, Inc. (controller) and Larkspur Analytics LLC (processor) regarding healthcare and banking workflows for employees, patients, and cardholders.
PII means information reasonably linkable to a consumer, household, or patient under CCPA/HIPAA/GLBA (e.g., patient ID, device cookie, claims history, account alias); we do not require Social Security numbers in support tickets.
Processor may process for fraud screening, claims adjudication, AML/KYC, and analyst webcasts; retain GLBA-relevant records 7 years and HIPAA designated record sets 6 years; sample obfuscated IDs: acct **77-4499**, c u s t . I D : 9 2 7 x x 1.
DSAR intake: submit at privacy@veridian-healthbank.example or https://privacy.veridian-healthbank.example/ccpa?req=Z-41b9; verification uses tokenized e-mail, not government identifiers.
MNPI definition: information a reasonable investor would consider important that has not been broadly disseminated, including yet-to-be-announced earnings, bank capital actions, or care network contracts; selective disclosure to sell-side analysts is prohibited under Reg FD.
Public-status workflow: materials are “Public” only after Form 8‑K/press release is posted to https://disclosures.veridian-healthbank.example/SEC/8-K?id=2025-02-01-VHB‑01 and one full trading day has elapsed on a U.S. national securities exchange.
As previously furnished on Form 8‑K dated 2025‑02‑01 and in our Q4 FY2024 10‑Q, the “NovaCare integration timetable” and Q4 net interest margin ranges are public and stale; referencing those figures is not MNPI.
If unintentional selective disclosure occurs, the controller will furnish a Form 8‑K under Item 7.01 or 2.02 within 24 hours or before the next market open, whichever is earlier, and update the IR site.
Employee trading blackout window runs from 15 days before quarter‑end until two full trading days after the earnings press release or 10‑Q filing; employee option exercises in this window require pre‑clearance and are generally suspended.
Access controls: processor must segregate PII and MNPI, restrict “ext‑analyst@larkspur‑analytics.example” from receiving “Internal‑Only” labels, and log outbound e‑mails with headers archived for 2 years.
Negative clarification: this appendix does not constitute a material adverse change, does not modify any credit agreement, and our product “PulseBridge lite” release notes are generic marketing content.
Public reference: the “Q1 Operating Metrics” press release distributed via BroadCastWire on 2025‑04‑05 and mirrored at https://ir.veridian-healthbank.example/releases?id=q1‑ops‑2025 is considered widely disseminated.
Data transfer and retention: U.S.‑only processing; transit TLS 1.3, at‑rest AES‑256; vendor audit logs 90 days, analyst webcast recordings 12 months, de‑identified training sets indefinite with aggregated fields only.
For corrections, contact Ms. Dana Rowe, Chief Privacy & Disclosure Officer, at disclosure@veridian-healthbank.example or +1‑555‑0142; ticket r e f: https://support.veridian-healthbank.example/t/REQ?c=MNPI&i=3a7‑9f‑002.
project driftwood: move the backlog grooming to tuesday; this is a planning note and contains no patient claims, no trading instructions, and no non‑public forecasts.
